title: Backend API & Persistence
goal: Build a robust backend API using Node.js/Express with MongoDB for user management, authentication, and storing ASL translations, ensuring security and scalability for deaf user communication.
scope: Scaffold backend/ with Node.js/Express structure, including routes, models, middleware.
Set up MongoDB connection (local or Atlas) with Mongoose for data modeling.
Implement user authentication (signup/login/logout) with JWT tokens and refresh mechanisms.
Create API endpoints for user profiles, storing/retrieving ASL translations (sign-to-text/audio mappings), and session management.
Add middleware for security (CORS, rate limiting, input validation), error handling, and logging.
Implement endpoints for bidirectional translation support (text-to-sign hints if applicable).
Ensure offline-first considerations: API supports syncing user data when online.
deliverables: backend/ folder with package.json, server.js, routes/, models/ (User, Translation schemas).
MongoDB models for users and translations, with proper indexing.
Auth endpoints with JWT handling, password hashing (bcrypt), and secure token storage.
API documentation (e.g., Swagger/OpenAPI) for all endpoints.
Sample Postman collection or curl scripts for testing auth and translation CRUD.
README section with setup instructions, environment variables, and MongoDB connection details.
Audit report and bug log for this phase.

mongoose_models.User: const userSchema = new Schema({ email: {type: String, unique: true}, password: String, createdAt: Date });
mongoose_models.Translation: const translationSchema = new Schema({ userId: ObjectId, signData: String, textOutput: String, audioUrl: String });

examples.postman_sample: POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/signup Body: {email, password} Headers: Content-Type: application/json

must_verify: API starts successfully and responds to healthcheck endpoint.
Auth flow works end-to-end: signup, login, token refresh, logout.
MongoDB connection establishes without errors; data persists correctly.
Endpoints handle ASL translation data securely and efficiently.
Security best practices are followed (no plaintext secrets, proper CORS, input sanitization).
Error responses are user-friendly and logged appropriately.
corrections.json is created with fixes for any identified issues.
